statement: Offering users more choices and options is always better.
why_it_spreads: Intuition that flexibility and feature-richness equal value, plus stakeholder requests to add options.
evidence_against: NN/g on choice and Hick's Law (src-nngroup): more options increase decision time and can cause choice overload and abandonment.
Baymard (src-baymard): overly complex e-commerce flows and option overload contribute to checkout abandonment.
replacement_principle: Curate and prioritise choices around the user's likely goal, using sensible defaults and progressive disclosure, so flexibility does not become paralysis.
still_applies_when: Expert and configuration tools may genuinely need many options exposed
Removing meaningful choice to oversimplify can also harm users
